subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 1
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer, type your answer in the box, or fill in the blank. You may not use a calculator.

questions.questionText: Each square is 1 square unit. The shape is made of equal rows of squares. What is the area of the shape?
questions.explanation: There are 3 rows of 7 unit squares, so the area is 3 × 7 = 21 square units.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 3/8.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 2 of the 3 equal parts are shaded, so 2/3.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is split into 4 equal parts. What fraction names the area of ONE part?
questions.explanation: When a shape is split into 4 equal parts, each part is 1/4 of the whole area.

questions.questionText: Tara knows that 5 × 8 = 40. Which fact can help her find 6 × 8?
questions.explanation: 6 × 8 is one more group of 8 than 5 × 8, so 40 + 8 = 48. This uses the distributive property: 6 × 8 = (5 × 8) + (1 × 8).

questions.questionText: A teacher puts 9 chairs in each row and makes 7 rows. How many chairs are there in all?
questions.explanation: 7 rows × 9 chairs = 63 chairs.

questions.questionText: Marcus buys 6 packs of trading cards. Each pack has 8 cards. He gives 9 cards to a friend. How many cards does Marcus have left?
questions.explanation: First 6 × 8 = 48 cards. Then 48 − 9 = 39 cards left. The distractor 48 is the total before giving cards away.

questions.questionText: A bakery had 7 boxes of 6 cupcakes. In the morning they sold 15 cupcakes. How many cupcakes are left?
questions.explanation: 7 × 6 = 42 cupcakes to start. Then 42 − 15 = 27 cupcakes left.

questions.questionText: Look at the pattern: 4, 8, 12, 16, ___. What number comes next?
questions.explanation: The numbers increase by 4 each time. 16 + 4 = 20.

questions.questionText: Select ALL statements that are ALWAYS true.
options: The product of an even number and any whole number is even
The product of two odd numbers is even
The sum of two odd numbers is even
questions.explanation: Even × any whole number is even (4 × 3 = 12). Odd + odd is even (3 + 5 = 8). But odd × odd is odd (3 × 5 = 15), and multiples of 5 can be even (such as 10), so the last two are false.

questions.questionText: Select ALL expressions that are equal to 36.
A coach divides 56 players into teams of 8 players each. How many teams are there?
questions.explanation: 56 ÷ 8 = 7 teams.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 6 × 8 = 48. To make 48 = ▢ + 20, the blank must be 48 − 20 = 28.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: If a number divided by 9 equals 4, then the number is 9 × 4 = 36.

questions.questionText: What is 365 + 247?
What number do these base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 5 hundreds, 0 tens, and 7 ones make 507.

questions.questionText: What is 549 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: To round to the nearest hundred, look at the tens digit. It is 4, so round DOWN: 549 rounds to 500. Do not be tricked by the 9 in the ones place.

questions.questionText: A school had 705 pencils. Students used 268 of them. How many pencils are left?
What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 4 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 4/6.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 8 equal parts. The point is at the third mark, which is 3/8.

questions.questionText: On a number line from 0 to 1, Point A is at 1/3 and Point B is at 1/4. Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: With numerator 1, the fraction with the SMALLER denominator is larger because the whole is cut into fewer, bigger pieces. Thirds are bigger than fourths, so 1/3 > 1/4, and A is to the right of B.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.explanation: Bar B shows 3 of 6 parts shaded, the same amount as 1 of 2. So 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 4 equal parts are shaded, so 3/4.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to the whole number 1?
questions.explanation: When all 3 of 3 equal parts are shaded, the whole circle is filled. 3/3 = 1.

questions.questionText: There are 15 grapes on a plate. Maya eats 1/3 of them. How many grapes does Maya eat?
questions.explanation: To find 1/3 of 15, split into 3 equal groups: 15 ÷ 3 = 5 grapes.

questions.questionText: Select ALL fractions that are equal to 1/2.
questions.explanation: 2/4, 3/6, and 4/8 all equal 1/2. But 2/3 is more than 1/2.

questions.questionText: Greg ate 2/4 of a small pizza. Dana ate 2/8 of a same-size pizza. Who ate more pizza?
questions.explanation: Both ate 2 pieces, but fourths are bigger than eighths, so 2/4 > 2/8. Greg ate more.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 3 equal parts. The point is at the second mark, which is 2/3.

questions.questionText: What is the area of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 8 × 4 = 32 square cm.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is 9 feet long and 2 feet wide. What is its PERIMETER?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 9 + 2 + 9 + 2 = 22 ft. The distractor 18 is the AREA (9 × 2), a common area-vs-perimeter mix-up.

questions.questionText: A square garden has sides that are each 6 meters long. What is the perimeter of the garden, in meters?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides. Perimeter = 6 + 6 + 6 + 6 = 24 meters.

questions.questionText: Recess starts at 10:40. It lasts 35 minutes. What time does recess end?
questions.explanation: 10:40 + 35 minutes crosses the hour. 10:40 + 20 = 11:00, then 15 more minutes makes 11:15.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is just past 3 and the minute hand points to 3, which is 15 minutes. The time is 3:15.

questions.questionText: A pitcher holds 2 liters of juice. How many liters of juice are in 4 full pitchers? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: 4 pitchers × 2 liters = 8 liters.

questions.questionText: Each ⭐ stands for 5 tickets sold. How many MORE tickets were sold on Friday than on Thursday?
questions.explanation: Friday: 7 × 5 = 35 tickets. Thursday: 3 × 5 = 15 tickets. 35 − 15 = 20 more tickets. The distractor 4 is the difference in stars, not tickets.

questions.questionText: Select ALL shapes that are quadrilaterals.
questions.explanation: Rectangles, rhombuses, and trapezoids all have exactly 4 sides, so they are quadrilaterals. A triangle has 3 sides.
